meta.title: EFU Reality · The House
meta.description: EFU Reality is our martial-arts talent-search format: the House, the Rules, the Mentors, the Contestants, and the Prize — everything you need to know about the 2026 season.

hero.tagline: EFU Reality is our martial-arts talent-search format. A multi-week competition where contestants are tested in the cage and in everyday life — through challenges, tasks, and real confrontations.

house.title: The House
house.intro: For the 2026 season, contestants move into the EFU House: a fully equipped training and living complex under 24-hour observation. The House is more than accommodation — it is the show's second stage: shared training, kitchen arguments, dawn runs, and house challenges are all part of the format.

nappali.name: Living Room
nappali.desc: The central space of the House. It hosts shared meals, jury briefings, house-challenge announcements, and off-the-record conversations — most of the drama is born here.

konyha.name: Kitchen
konyha.desc: The heart of the House. The menu designed by the nutrition advisor and the chef supports regeneration and season-long preparation. Cooking together is a key team-building moment.

halok.name: Bedrooms
halok.desc: Separate male and female bedrooms with personal lockers and headboard-mounted cameras. Rest time is strictly protected — the House is built for balanced preparation, not sleep deprivation.

edzoterem.name: Gym
edzoterem.desc: Full MMA equipment: cage, striking and grappling mats, heavy bags, strength stations, recovery corner. Mentors can use the space 24/7 — training sessions are mandatory.

kert.name: Garden
kert.desc: Grass pitch for sprint and endurance work, outdoor sauna, hot tub, and an open terrace for shared conversations. The garden also hosts outdoor challenges.

egyeb.name: Other rooms
egyeb.desc: Sauna and hot tub for recovery, dedicated physio and massage room, and a 24/7 medical bay. The full House infrastructure exists to support safe, professional preparation.

rules.title: Rules
rules.intro: EFU Reality is built on EFU's own rule set. The same core principles apply in the House and in the cage: respect, safety, fair play.

items.title: Move-in and selection
points: Mentors set the in-House ranking within 48 hours of move-in
The initial ranking is based on performance showcases, interviews, and physical tests
Lowest-ranked contestant of week one faces immediate elimination

items.title: Weekly challenges
points: At the start of each week mentors announce a public house challenge
The challenge is mandatory — every contestant performs in front of the full House
The jury (mentors + 1 guest) scores; an in-House vote supplements the final ranking

items.title: Cage matchups
points: Matches follow the EFU Ruleset (Stand-Up or Hybrid) only
Mandatory medical check before every matchup
In case of dispute, the referee's decision is cross-checked with in-House scorecards

items.title: Elimination and return
points: The lowest-ranked contestant must fight a duel in the next match
The duel's winner stays in the House; the loser may be offered a return slot by the mentors
Only an aggregate of in-House ranking and audience votes can place someone in the final

items.title: House conduct
points: Aggression, harassment, or damage to the House infrastructure leads to immediate removal
Alcohol and substance use are banned — regular testing throughout the season
Privacy protection: bedroom camera feeds are accessible only to the production crew

mentors.title: Mentors
mentors.intro: EFU Reality's mentors are among the most respected coaches, former champions, and sport psychologists of the Central-European fight scene. They live in the House for the entire season and prepare with the contestants for every challenge.

members.name: István Kovács
members.bio: Three-time Hungarian champion with 18 years of international experience. One of the architects of the EFU Stand-Up rule set.

members.name: László Nagy
members.bio: BJJ black belt, former IBJJF European champion. Leads the limited-grappling sessions of the Hybrid rule set in the House.

members.name: Beatrix Tóth
members.bio: Sport psychologist and mental coach for numerous national-team athletes. Specializes in conflict resolution, recovery, and pressure handling in the House.

members.name: Máté Varga
members.bio: Strength coach and former international rugby player. Responsible for season preparation, recovery protocols, and nutrition.

contestants.title: Contestants
contestants.intro: The identities of the 2026 season contestants will be revealed when the House doors open. Until then, contestants are represented by silhouettes — everyone in the House starts on equal footing.
contestants.teaser: When the season opens, real faces and real stories replace the silhouettes. Subscribe to be the first to know.
contestants.notifyMe: Notify me when contestants are revealed

prize.prelaunchBody: EFU Reality's exact prize structure will be revealed when the House opens. Subscribe to be the first to know.
prize.launchMainNote: The season's winner receives a 10-million-forint main prize.
prize.launchCareerIntro: EFU Reality is about more than the winner. The strongest contestants may receive the chance to:
launchCareerBullets: appear at EFU Fight Night events
participate in interviews and promotional content
build a long-term partnership inside the Elite Fight Universe system
prize.swapNote: Pre-launch: "Coming soon…". At launch: HUF 10M + EFU fighter contract.

applyCta.title: You could be the next EFU fighter
applyCta.body: Apply for the 2026 season. The House is waiting.

footer.disclaimer: The exact House regulations, jury composition, and match formats are finalised before the official season start.
